登渡神社 | image = File:Towatari-jinja 06.jpg | caption = Towatari Shrine's ''[[Heiden (Shinto)|''hei'']]-[[Haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]'' | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|35.612306|N|140.106278|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|WD=Q2842779}}; {{ill|Amenohiwashi|WD=Q10939697}}; {{ill|Kamimusubi|WD=Q3275901}}; {{ill|Takamimusubi|WD=Q125400}} | founded_by = [[:ja:千葉定胤|Chiba Sadatane]] | established = [[:en:1644 (year)|1644]] ([[:en:Shōhō|Shōhō]] 1) | date_destroyed = | location = 3-3-8, Nobuto, [[:en:Chūō-ku, Chiba|Chūō-ku]], [[:en:Chiba (city)|Chiba]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]] | website = {{URL|https://www.towatarijinjya.com/}} | architecture_style = | festival = 4–6 September | leadership = | location = {{ill|Chuo|WD=Q1075869}} }} '''Towatari Shrine''' (登渡神社, ''Towatari-jinja''), also known as '''Nobuto Shrine''' (登戸神社, ''Nobuto-jinja''), is a [[Shinto]] [[:en:Shinto shrine|shrine]] located in Nobuto, [[:en:Chūō-ku, Chiba|Chūō-ku]], [[:en:Chiba (city)|Chiba City]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]], Japan. Originally a branch [[:en:Buddhist temples in Japan|temple]] of Kongojū-ji, a temple to the [[:en:Buddhism in Japan|Buddhist]] [[:en:Deva (Buddhism)|deity]] [[:en:Myōken|Myōken]] founded by the [[Chiba clan]] (modern [[Chiba Shrine]]), it was converted into a Shinto shrine during the mid-19th century. ==Deities== The shrine is dedicated to the three deities of creation (造化三神, ''zōka sanshin'') of classical [[Japanese mythology]]: [[Ame-no-Minakanushi]], [[Takamimusubi]], and [[Kamimusubi]]. Enshrined together with them is [[:ja:天日鷲神|1=Otori-sama|lt=Otori-sama|2=ja|3=天日鷲神|4=zh|5=天日鷲神|6=ko|7=아메노히와시노카미|Ame-no-Hiwashi]], originally the deity of a nearby shrine that was [[Shrine Consolidation Policy|merged]] into Nobuto Shrine in 1908 ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji]] 41).{{cite web|title=登渡神社について|url=https://www.towatarijinjya.com/jinjya|accessdate=2020-11-16|website=Towatari Shrine Official Website|language=Japanese}} ==History== [[File:Bay of Noboto.jpg|left|thumb|270x270px|''Bay of Noboto'' (Nobuto) by [[:en:Katsushika Hokusai|Katsushika Hokusai]]]] The shrine was originally founded in 1644 ([[:en:Shōhō|Shōhō]] 1) by [[:ja:千葉定胤|Chiba Sadatane]], 33rd head of the [[Chiba clan]], as a Buddhist temple named '''Hakujasan Shinkō-in Jōin-ji''' (白蛇山真光院定胤寺). It was a branch temple or ''matsuji'' (末寺) of Kongojū-ji (also known as Chiba Myōken-ji), a temple dedicated to [[:en:Myōken|Myōken]], the Buddhist deification of the [[:en:Big Dipper|Big Dipper]] and/or the [[:en:Polaris|northern pole star]] revered by the Chiba clan as their patron deity (what is today [[Chiba Shrine]]).{{cite book |editor=Chiba Prefecture |title=千葉県誌 巻上 (Chiba-ken shi, vol. 1) |date=1919 |publisher=Tadaya Shoten |pages=521 |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/960652/297}}{{cite book |last1=Nozaki |first1=Sabun |title=日本名勝地誌 第二編 (Nihon meishōchi shi, vol. 2) |date=1901 |publisher=Hakubunkan |page=166 |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=PkxMpcnBkZQC&dq=%E7%99%BB%E6%B8%A1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE&pg=PP200}}{{cite web |title=登渡神社 (Towatari-jinja) |url=http://www.jinja.ne.jp/ycDB/Board.cgi/001/db/ycDB-pc-jinja_detail.html?mode:search=1&search:method=1&search:field=jinja_id&search:expr=^$&search:word=1008 |website=神社のひろば 神社探検隊！ |publisher=Chiba-ken Jinjachō |accessdate=2020-11-18|language=Japanese}} In 1867 ([[:en:Keiō|Keiō]] 3), the temple became a Shinto shrine dedicated to the [[Kami|gods]] [[Ame-no-Minakanushi]] (a deity in Japanese mythology conflated with Myōken), [[Takamimusubi]], and [[Kamimusubi]] and renamed "Towatari Shrine". In 1990 ([[:en:Heisei|Heisei]] 2), the shrine's ''[[honden]]'' (dating from 1860) was moved to its present location and a new [[Heiden (Shinto)|''hei'']]-''[[Haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]'' constructed in front of it. ==Structures== *'''''Honden''''' :The ''honden'' was originally Jōin-ji's former [[:en:Main Hall (Japanese Buddhism)|main hall]], constructed in 1860 by a craftsman named Monjirō (紋次郎) from the [[:ja:八木村 (千葉県)|village of Yagi]] in [[:ja:葛飾郡|Katsushika District]], [[:en:Shimōsa Province|Shimōsa Province]] (part of the modern city of [[:en:Nagareyama|Nagareyama]], Chiba Prefecture).{{efn|Other notable projects by this artisan include the former main hall (the current Shaka-dō (釈迦堂) or Hall of [[:en:Gautama Buddha|Shakyamuni Tathāgata]]) of [[:en:Narita-san|Shinshō-ji]] in [[:en:Narita, Chiba|Narita]] (built 1858){{cite book |author1=Shiritsu Narita Toshokan |title=成田山通志 (Narita-san Tsushi) |date=1911 |publisher=Shiritsu Narita Toshokan |page=76 |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=QgNjpoXOSZMC}} and the former main hall of [[:ja:深川不動堂|Fukagawa Fudō-dō]] (a branch of Shinshō-ji) in [[:en:Fukagawa, Tokyo|Fukagawa]], [[:en:Kōtō|Kōtō City]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]] - originally a sanctuary dedicated to the [[:en:Bodhisattva|Bodhisattva]] [[:en:Kṣitigarbha|Kṣitigarbha]] (地蔵堂, Jizō-dō) in [[:ja:|Ryūfuku-ji]] in what is now [[:en:Inzai, Chiba|Inzai]] built somewhere between 1861 and 1864{{cite web |title=深川不動堂旧本堂 |url=https://www.city.koto.lg.jp/103020/bunkasports/bunka/bunkazaisiseki/kenzobutsu/17348.html |website=Kōtō City Official Website |language=Japanese}}{{cite web |title=今むかし④～本堂移築 |url=http://fukagawafudou.jugem.jp/?eid=314 |website=Narita-san Fukagawa Fudō-dō |accessdate=2020-11-18 |language=Japanese}}.}}{{cite book |editor=Chiba-shi Shi Hensan Iinkai |title=千葉市誌 (Chiba-shi Shi) |date=1958 |publisher=Chiba-shi Shi Hensan Iinkai |page=175}} The bas-reliefs decorating the building were carved by [[:ja:立川和四郎|Tatekawa Washirō Tomimasa]] (立川和四郎富昌, the 2nd generation of the Tatekawa Washirō line of craftsmen) from Suwa District in [[:en:Shinano Province|Shinano Province]] (modern [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]]) and/or his son Tomishige (富重).{{cite web |title=小壁嵌板彫刻（市指定文化財） |url=https://www.city.chiba.jp/kyoiku/shogaigakushu/bunkazai/kokabehameita.html |website=Chiba City Official Website |accessdate=2020-11-18 |language=Japanese}} The reliefs were designated [[Tangible Cultural Property (Japan)|tangible cultural properties]] by Chiba City in 1967 ([[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] 42). ==See also== *[[Chiba Shrine]] *[[Chiba clan]] *[[Taira clan]] *[[:en:Shimōsa Province|Shimōsa Province]] ==Notes== {{notelist}} ==References== {{reflist|2}} ==External links== *[https://www.towatarijinjya.com/ Official website of Towatari Shrine] (Japanese) ‎]]]] {{Myōken Faith}} {{draft categories| [[Category:17th-century Shinto shrines]] [[Category:1600s establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Chiba (city)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinbutsu shūgō]] [[Category:Shinbutsu bunri]] [[Category:Myōken Faith]] }} [[en:Towatari Shrine]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] {{Commons category|Towatari-jinja}} [[ja:登渡神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11579153}} [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:LINKED FROM WIKIDATA DO NOT OVERWRITE]] [[Category:qq]]{{wikidata link|Q1}}[[Category:qq]]

In 1990 ([[:en:Heisei|Heisei]] 2), the shrine's ''[[honden]]'' (dating from 1860) was moved to its present location and a new [[Heiden (Shinto)|''hei'']]-''[[Haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]'' constructed in front of it. ==Structures== *'''''Honden''''' :The ''honden'' was originally Jōin-ji's former [[:en:Main Hall (Japanese Buddhism)|main hall]], constructed in 1860 by a craftsman named Monjirō (紋次郎) from the [[:ja:八木村 (千葉県)|village of Yagi]] in [[:ja:葛飾郡|Katsushika District]], [[:en:Shimōsa Province|Shimōsa Province]] (part of the modern city of [[:en:Nagareyama|Nagareyama]], Chiba Prefecture).{{efn|Other notable projects by this artisan include the former main hall (the current Shaka-dō (釈迦堂) or Hall of [[:en:Gautama Buddha|Shakyamuni Tathāgata]]) of [[:en:Narita-san|Shinshō-ji]] in [[:en:Narita, Chiba|Narita]] (built 1858){{cite book |author1=Shiritsu Narita Toshokan |title=成田山通志 (Narita-san Tsushi) |date=1911 |publisher=Shiritsu Narita Toshokan |page=76 |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=QgNjpoXOSZMC}} and the former main hall of [[:ja:深川不動堂|Fukagawa Fudō-dō]] (a branch of Shinshō-ji) in [[:en:Fukagawa, Tokyo|Fukagawa]], [[:en:Kōtō|Kōtō City]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]] - originally a sanctuary dedicated to the [[:en:Bodhisattva|Bodhisattva]] [[:en:Kṣitigarbha|Kṣitigarbha]] (地蔵堂, Jizō-dō) in [[:ja:|Ryūfuku-ji]] in what is now [[:en:Inzai, Chiba|Inzai]] built somewhere between 1861 and 1864{{cite web |title=深川不動堂旧本堂 |url=https://www.city.koto.lg.jp/103020/bunkasports/bunka/bunkazaisiseki/kenzobutsu/17348.html |website=Kōtō City Official Website |language=Japanese}}{{cite web |title=今むかし④～本堂移築 |url=http://fukagawafudou.jugem.jp/?eid=314 |website=Narita-san Fukagawa Fudō-dō |accessdate=2020-11-18 |language=Japanese}}.}}{{cite book |editor=Chiba-shi Shi Hensan Iinkai |title=千葉市誌 (Chiba-shi Shi) |date=1958 |publisher=Chiba-shi Shi Hensan Iinkai |page=175}} The bas-reliefs decorating the building were carved by [[:ja:立川和四郎|Tatekawa Washirō Tomimasa]] (立川和四郎富昌, the 2nd generation of the Tatekawa Washirō line of craftsmen) from Suwa District in [[:en:Shinano Province|Shinano Province]] (modern [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]]) and/or his son Tomishige (富重).{{cite web |title=小壁嵌板彫刻（市指定文化財） |url=https://www.city.chiba.jp/kyoiku/shogaigakushu/bunkazai/kokabehameita.html |website=Chiba City Official Website |accessdate=2020-11-18 |language=Japanese}} The reliefs were designated [[Tangible Cultural Property (Japan)|tangible cultural properties]] by Chiba City in 1967 ([[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] 42). ==See also== *[[Chiba Shrine]] *[[Chiba clan]] *[[Taira clan]] *[[:en:Shimōsa Province|Shimōsa Province]] ==Notes== {{notelist}} ==References== {{reflist|2}} ==External links== *[https://www.towatarijinjya.com/ Official website of Towatari Shrine] (Japanese) ‎]]]] {{Myōken Faith}} {{draft categories| [[Category:17th-century Shinto shrines]] [[Category:1600s establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Chiba (city)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinbutsu shūgō]] [[Category:Shinbutsu bunri]] [[Category:Myōken Faith]] }} [[sn:commons:Category:Towatari-jinja]][[en:Towatari Shrine]][[ja:登渡神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11579153]] Wikidata name: Towatari Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:02, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Shinto shrine in Chiba, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Towatari Shrine
登渡神社 | image = File:Towatari-jinja 06.jpg | caption = Towatari Shrine's ''[[Heiden (Shinto)|''hei'']]-[[Haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]'' | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|35.612306|N|140.106278|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|WD=Q2842779}}; {{ill|Amenohiwashi|WD=Q10939697}}; {{ill|Kamimusubi|WD=Q3275901}}; {{ill|Takamimusubi|WD=Q125400}} | founded_by = [[:ja:千葉定胤|Chiba Sadatane]] | established = [[:en:1644 (year)|1644]] ([[:en:Shōhō|Shōhō]] 1) | date_destroyed = | location = 3-3-8, Nobuto, [[:en:Chūō-ku, Chiba|Chūō-ku]], [[:en:Chiba (city)|Chiba]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]] | website = {{URL|https://www.towatarijinjya.com/}} | architecture_style = | festival = 4–6 September | leadership = | location = {{ill|Chuo|WD=Q1075869}} }} '''Towatari Shrine''' (登渡神社, ''Towatari-jinja''), also known as '''Nobuto Shrine''' (登戸神社, ''Nobuto-jinja''), is a [[Shinto]] [[:en:Shinto shrine|shrine]] located in Nobuto, [[:en:Chūō-ku, Chiba|Chūō-ku]], [[:en:Chiba (city)|Chiba City]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]], Japan. Originally a branch [[:en:Buddhist temples in Japan|temple]] of Kongojū-ji, a temple to the [[:en:Buddhism in Japan|Buddhist]] [[:en:Deva (Buddhism)|deity]] [[:en:Myōken|Myōken]] founded by the [[Chiba clan]] (modern [[Chiba Shrine]]), it was converted into a Shinto shrine during the mid-19th century. ==Deities== The shrine is dedicated to the three deities of creation (造化三神, ''zōka sanshin'') of classical [[Japanese mythology]]: [[Ame-no-Minakanushi]], [[Takamimusubi]], and [[Kamimusubi]]. Enshrined together with them is [[:ja:天日鷲神|1=Otori-sama|lt=Otori-sama|2=ja|3=天日鷲神|4=zh|5=天日鷲神|6=ko|7=아메노히와시노카미|Ame-no-Hiwashi]], originally the deity of a nearby shrine that was [[Shrine Consolidation Policy|merged]] into Nobuto Shrine in 1908 ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji]] 41).{{cite web|title=登渡神社について|url=https://www.towatarijinjya.com/jinjya|accessdate=2020-11-16|website=Towatari Shrine Official Website|language=Japanese}} ==History== [[File:Bay of Noboto.jpg|left|thumb|270x270px|''Bay of Noboto'' (Nobuto) by [[:en:Katsushika Hokusai|Katsushika Hokusai]]]] The shrine was originally founded in 1644 ([[:en:Shōhō|Shōhō]] 1) by [[:ja:千葉定胤|Chiba Sadatane]], 33rd head of the [[Chiba clan]], as a Buddhist temple named '''Hakujasan Shinkō-in Jōin-ji''' (白蛇山真光院定胤寺). It was a branch temple or ''matsuji'' (末寺) of Kongojū-ji (also known as Chiba Myōken-ji), a temple dedicated to [[:en:Myōken|Myōken]], the Buddhist deification of the [[:en:Big Dipper|Big Dipper]] and/or the [[:en:Polaris|northern pole star]] revered by the Chiba clan as their patron deity (what is today [[Chiba Shrine]]).{{cite book |editor=Chiba Prefecture |title=千葉県誌 巻上 (Chiba-ken shi, vol. 1) |date=1919 |publisher=Tadaya Shoten |pages=521 |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/960652/297}}{{cite book |last1=Nozaki |first1=Sabun |title=日本名勝地誌 第二編 (Nihon meishōchi shi, vol. 2) |date=1901 |publisher=Hakubunkan |page=166 |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=PkxMpcnBkZQC&dq=%E7%99%BB%E6%B8%A1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE&pg=PP200}}{{cite web |title=登渡神社 (Towatari-jinja) |url=http://www.jinja.ne.jp/ycDB/Board.cgi/001/db/ycDB-pc-jinja_detail.html?mode:search=1&search:method=1&search:field=jinja_id&search:expr=^$&search:word=1008 |website=神社のひろば 神社探検隊！ |publisher=Chiba-ken Jinjachō |accessdate=2020-11-18|language=Japanese}} In 1867 ([[:en:Keiō|Keiō]] 3), the temple became a Shinto shrine dedicated to the [[Kami|gods]] [[Ame-no-Minakanushi]] (a deity in Japanese mythology conflated with Myōken), [[Takamimusubi]], and [[Kamimusubi]] and renamed "Towatari Shrine". In 1990 ([[:en:Heisei|Heisei]] 2), the shrine's ''[[honden]]'' (dating from 1860) was moved to its present location and a new [[Heiden (Shinto)|''hei'']]-''[[Haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]'' constructed in front of it. ==Structures== *'''''Honden''''' :The ''honden'' was originally Jōin-ji's former [[:en:Main Hall (Japanese Buddhism)|main hall]], constructed in 1860 by a craftsman named Monjirō (紋次郎) from the [[:ja:八木村 (千葉県)|village of Yagi]] in [[:ja:葛飾郡|Katsushika District]], [[:en:Shimōsa Province|Shimōsa Province]] (part of the modern city of [[:en:Nagareyama|Nagareyama]], Chiba Prefecture).{{efn|Other notable projects by this artisan include the former main hall (the current Shaka-dō (釈迦堂) or Hall of [[:en:Gautama Buddha|Shakyamuni Tathāgata]]) of [[:en:Narita-san|Shinshō-ji]] in [[:en:Narita, Chiba|Narita]] (built 1858){{cite book |author1=Shiritsu Narita Toshokan |title=成田山通志 (Narita-san Tsushi) |date=1911 |publisher=Shiritsu Narita Toshokan |page=76 |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=QgNjpoXOSZMC}} and the former main hall of [[:ja:深川不動堂|Fukagawa Fudō-dō]] (a branch of Shinshō-ji) in [[:en:Fukagawa, Tokyo|Fukagawa]], [[:en:Kōtō|Kōtō City]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]] - originally a sanctuary dedicated to the [[:en:Bodhisattva|Bodhisattva]] [[:en:Kṣitigarbha|Kṣitigarbha]] (地蔵堂, Jizō-dō) in [[:ja:|Ryūfuku-ji]] in what is now [[:en:Inzai, Chiba|Inzai]] built somewhere between 1861 and 1864{{cite web |title=深川不動堂旧本堂 |url=https://www.city.koto.lg.jp/103020/bunkasports/bunka/bunkazaisiseki/kenzobutsu/17348.html |website=Kōtō City Official Website |language=Japanese}}{{cite web |title=今むかし④～本堂移築 |url=http://fukagawafudou.jugem.jp/?eid=314 |website=Narita-san Fukagawa Fudō-dō |accessdate=2020-11-18 |language=Japanese}}.}}{{cite book |editor=Chiba-shi Shi Hensan Iinkai |title=千葉市誌 (Chiba-shi Shi) |date=1958 |publisher=Chiba-shi Shi Hensan Iinkai |page=175}} The bas-reliefs decorating the building were carved by [[:ja:立川和四郎|Tatekawa Washirō Tomimasa]] (立川和四郎富昌, the 2nd generation of the Tatekawa Washirō line of craftsmen) from Suwa District in [[:en:Shinano Province|Shinano Province]] (modern [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]]) and/or his son Tomishige (富重).{{cite web |title=小壁嵌板彫刻（市指定文化財） |url=https://www.city.chiba.jp/kyoiku/shogaigakushu/bunkazai/kokabehameita.html |website=Chiba City Official Website |accessdate=2020-11-18 |language=Japanese}} The reliefs were designated [[Tangible Cultural Property (Japan)|tangible cultural properties]] by Chiba City in 1967 ([[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] 42). ==See also== *[[Chiba Shrine]] *[[Chiba clan]] *[[Taira clan]] *[[:en:Shimōsa Province|Shimōsa Province]] ==Notes== {{notelist}} ==References== {{reflist|2}} ==External links== *[https://www.towatarijinjya.com/ Official website of Towatari Shrine] (Japanese) ‎]]]] {{Myōken Faith}} {{draft categories| [[Category:17th-century Shinto shrines]] [[Category:1600s establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Chiba (city)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinbutsu shūgō]] [[Category:Shinbutsu bunri]] [[Category:Myōken Faith]] }} [[commons:Category:Towatari-jinja]][[en:Towatari Shrine]][[ja:登渡神社]] [[sn:commons:Category:Towatari-jinja]][[en:Towatari Shrine]][[ja:登渡神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11579153]] Wikidata name: Towatari Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:02, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Shinto shrine in Chiba, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Towatari Shrine
登渡神社 | image = File:Towatari-jinja 06.jpg | caption = Towatari Shrine's ''[[Heiden (Shinto)|''hei'']]-[[Haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]'' | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|35.612306|N|140.106278|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|WD=Q2842779}}; {{ill|Amenohiwashi|WD=Q10939697}}; {{ill|Kamimusubi|WD=Q3275901}}; {{ill|Takamimusubi|WD=Q125400}} | founded_by = [[:ja:千葉定胤|Chiba Sadatane]] | established = [[:en:1644 (year)|1644]] ([[:en:Shōhō|Shōhō]] 1) | date_destroyed = | location = 3-3-8, Nobuto, [[:en:Chūō-ku, Chiba|Chūō-ku]], [[:en:Chiba (city)|Chiba]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]] | website = {{URL|https://www.towatarijinjya.com/}} | architecture_style = | festival = 4–6 September | leadership = | location = {{ill|Chuo|WD=Q1075869}} }} '''Towatari Shrine''' (登渡神社, ''Towatari-jinja''), also known as '''Nobuto Shrine''' (登戸神社, ''Nobuto-jinja''), is a [[Shinto]] [[:en:Shinto shrine|shrine]] located in Nobuto, [[:en:Chūō-ku, Chiba|Chūō-ku]], [[:en:Chiba (city)|Chiba City]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]], Japan. Originally a branch [[:en:Buddhist temples in Japan|temple]] of Kongojū-ji, a temple to the [[:en:Buddhism in Japan|Buddhist]] [[:en:Deva (Buddhism)|deity]] [[:en:Myōken|Myōken]] founded by the [[Chiba clan]] (modern [[Chiba Shrine]]), it was converted into a Shinto shrine during the mid-19th century. ==Deities== The shrine is dedicated to the three deities of creation (造化三神, ''zōka sanshin'') of classical [[Japanese mythology]]: [[Ame-no-Minakanushi]], [[Takamimusubi]], and [[Kamimusubi]]. Enshrined together with them is [[:ja:天日鷲神|1=Otori-sama|lt=Otori-sama|2=ja|3=天日鷲神|4=zh|5=天日鷲神|6=ko|7=아메노히와시노카미|Ame-no-Hiwashi]], originally the deity of a nearby shrine that was [[Shrine Consolidation Policy|merged]] into Nobuto Shrine in 1908 ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji]] 41).{{cite web|title=登渡神社について|url=https://www.towatarijinjya.com/jinjya|accessdate=2020-11-16|website=Towatari Shrine Official Website|language=Japanese}} ==History== [[File:Bay of Noboto.jpg|left|thumb|270x270px|''Bay of Noboto'' (Nobuto) by [[:en:Katsushika Hokusai|Katsushika Hokusai]]]] The shrine was originally founded in 1644 ([[:en:Shōhō|Shōhō]] 1) by [[:ja:千葉定胤|Chiba Sadatane]], 33rd head of the [[Chiba clan]], as a Buddhist temple named '''Hakujasan Shinkō-in Jōin-ji''' (白蛇山真光院定胤寺). It was a branch temple or ''matsuji'' (末寺) of Kongojū-ji (also known as Chiba Myōken-ji), a temple dedicated to [[:en:Myōken|Myōken]], the Buddhist deification of the [[:en:Big Dipper|Big Dipper]] and/or the [[:en:Polaris|northern pole star]] revered by the Chiba clan as their patron deity (what is today [[Chiba Shrine]]).{{cite book |editor=Chiba Prefecture |title=千葉県誌 巻上 (Chiba-ken shi, vol. 1) |date=1919 |publisher=Tadaya Shoten |pages=521 |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/960652/297}}{{cite book |last1=Nozaki |first1=Sabun |title=日本名勝地誌 第二編 (Nihon meishōchi shi, vol. 2) |date=1901 |publisher=Hakubunkan |page=166 |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=PkxMpcnBkZQC&dq=%E7%99%BB%E6%B8%A1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE&pg=PP200}}{{cite web |title=登渡神社 (Towatari-jinja) |url=http://www.jinja.ne.jp/ycDB/Board.cgi/001/db/ycDB-pc-jinja_detail.html?mode:search=1&search:method=1&search:field=jinja_id&search:expr=^$&search:word=1008 |website=神社のひろば 神社探検隊！ |publisher=Chiba-ken Jinjachō |accessdate=2020-11-18|language=Japanese}} In 1867 ([[:en:Keiō|Keiō]] 3), the temple became a Shinto shrine dedicated to the [[Kami|gods]] [[Ame-no-Minakanushi]] (a deity in Japanese mythology conflated with Myōken), [[Takamimusubi]], and [[Kamimusubi]] and renamed "Towatari Shrine". In 1990 ([[:en:Heisei|Heisei]] 2), the shrine's ''[[honden]]'' (dating from 1860) was moved to its present location and a new [[Heiden (Shinto)|''hei'']]-''[[Haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]'' constructed in front of it. ==Structures== *'''''Honden''''' :The ''honden'' was originally Jōin-ji's former [[:en:Main Hall (Japanese Buddhism)|main hall]], constructed in 1860 by a craftsman named Monjirō (紋次郎) from the [[:ja:八木村 (千葉県)|village of Yagi]] in [[:ja:葛飾郡|Katsushika District]], [[:en:Shimōsa Province|Shimōsa Province]] (part of the modern city of [[:en:Nagareyama|Nagareyama]], Chiba Prefecture).{{efn|Other notable projects by this artisan include the former main hall (the current Shaka-dō (釈迦堂) or Hall of [[:en:Gautama Buddha|Shakyamuni Tathāgata]]) of [[:en:Narita-san|Shinshō-ji]] in [[:en:Narita, Chiba|Narita]] (built 1858){{cite book |author1=Shiritsu Narita Toshokan |title=成田山通志 (Narita-san Tsushi) |date=1911 |publisher=Shiritsu Narita Toshokan |page=76 |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=QgNjpoXOSZMC}} and the former main hall of [[:ja:深川不動堂|Fukagawa Fudō-dō]] (a branch of Shinshō-ji) in [[:en:Fukagawa, Tokyo|Fukagawa]], [[:en:Kōtō|Kōtō City]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]] - originally a sanctuary dedicated to the [[:en:Bodhisattva|Bodhisattva]] [[:en:Kṣitigarbha|Kṣitigarbha]] (地蔵堂, Jizō-dō) in [[:ja:|Ryūfuku-ji]] in what is now [[:en:Inzai, Chiba|Inzai]] built somewhere between 1861 and 1864{{cite web |title=深川不動堂旧本堂 |url=https://www.city.koto.lg.jp/103020/bunkasports/bunka/bunkazaisiseki/kenzobutsu/17348.html |website=Kōtō City Official Website |language=Japanese}}{{cite web |title=今むかし④～本堂移築 |url=http://fukagawafudou.jugem.jp/?eid=314 |website=Narita-san Fukagawa Fudō-dō |accessdate=2020-11-18 |language=Japanese}}.}}{{cite book |editor=Chiba-shi Shi Hensan Iinkai |title=千葉市誌 (Chiba-shi Shi) |date=1958 |publisher=Chiba-shi Shi Hensan Iinkai |page=175}} The bas-reliefs decorating the building were carved by [[:ja:立川和四郎|Tatekawa Washirō Tomimasa]] (立川和四郎富昌, the 2nd generation of the Tatekawa Washirō line of craftsmen) from Suwa District in [[:en:Shinano Province|Shinano Province]] (modern [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]]) and/or his son Tomishige (富重).{{cite web |title=小壁嵌板彫刻（市指定文化財） |url=https://www.city.chiba.jp/kyoiku/shogaigakushu/bunkazai/kokabehameita.html |website=Chiba City Official Website |accessdate=2020-11-18 |language=Japanese}} The reliefs were designated [[Tangible Cultural Property (Japan)|tangible cultural properties]] by Chiba City in 1967 ([[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] 42). ==See also== *[[Chiba Shrine]] *[[Chiba clan]] *[[Taira clan]] *[[:en:Shimōsa Province|Shimōsa Province]] ==Notes== {{notelist}} ==References== {{reflist|2}} ==External links== *[https://www.towatarijinjya.com/ Official website of Towatari Shrine] (Japanese) ‎]]]] {{Myōken Faith}} {{draft categories| [[Category:17th-century Shinto shrines]] [[Category:1600s establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Chiba (city)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinbutsu shūgō]] [[Category:Shinbutsu bunri]] [[Category:Myōken Faith]] }} [[en:Towatari Shrine]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] {{Commons category|Towatari-jinja}} [[ja:登渡神社]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] {{wikidata link|Q11579153}}

front of it. ==Structures== *'''''Honden''''' :The ''honden'' was originally Jōin-ji's former [[:en:Main Hall (Japanese Buddhism)|main hall]], constructed in 1860 by a craftsman named Monjirō (紋次郎) from the [[:ja:八木村 (千葉県)|village of Yagi]] in [[:ja:葛飾郡|Katsushika District]], [[:en:Shimōsa Province|Shimōsa Province]] (part of the modern city of [[:en:Nagareyama|Nagareyama]], Chiba Prefecture).{{efn|Other notable projects by this artisan include the former main hall (the current Shaka-dō (釈迦堂) or Hall of [[:en:Gautama Buddha|Shakyamuni Tathāgata]]) of [[:en:Narita-san|Shinshō-ji]] in [[:en:Narita, Chiba|Narita]] (built 1858){{cite book |author1=Shiritsu Narita Toshokan |title=成田山通志 (Narita-san Tsushi) |date=1911 |publisher=Shiritsu Narita Toshokan |page=76 |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=QgNjpoXOSZMC}} and the former main hall of [[:ja:深川不動堂|Fukagawa Fudō-dō]] (a branch of Shinshō-ji) in [[:en:Fukagawa, Tokyo|Fukagawa]], [[:en:Kōtō|Kōtō City]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]] - originally a sanctuary dedicated to the [[:en:Bodhisattva|Bodhisattva]] [[:en:Kṣitigarbha|Kṣitigarbha]] (地蔵堂, Jizō-dō) in [[:ja:|Ryūfuku-ji]] in what is now [[:en:Inzai, Chiba|Inzai]] built somewhere between 1861 and 1864{{cite web |title=深川不動堂旧本堂 |url=https://www.city.koto.lg.jp/103020/bunkasports/bunka/bunkazaisiseki/kenzobutsu/17348.html |website=Kōtō City Official Website |language=Japanese}}{{cite web |title=今むかし④～本堂移築 |url=http://fukagawafudou.jugem.jp/?eid=314 |website=Narita-san Fukagawa Fudō-dō |accessdate=2020-11-18 |language=Japanese}}.}}{{cite book |editor=Chiba-shi Shi Hensan Iinkai |title=千葉市誌 (Chiba-shi Shi) |date=1958 |publisher=Chiba-shi Shi Hensan Iinkai |page=175}} The bas-reliefs decorating the building were carved by [[:ja:立川和四郎|Tatekawa Washirō Tomimasa]] (立川和四郎富昌, the 2nd generation of the Tatekawa Washirō line of craftsmen) from Suwa District in [[:en:Shinano Province|Shinano Province]] (modern [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]]) and/or his son Tomishige (富重).{{cite web |title=小壁嵌板彫刻（市指定文化財） |url=https://www.city.chiba.jp/kyoiku/shogaigakushu/bunkazai/kokabehameita.html |website=Chiba City Official Website |accessdate=2020-11-18 |language=Japanese}} The reliefs were designated [[Tangible Cultural Property (Japan)|tangible cultural properties]] by Chiba City in 1967 ([[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] 42). ==See also== *[[Chiba Shrine]] *[[Chiba clan]] *[[Taira clan]] *[[:en:Shimōsa Province|Shimōsa Province]] ==Notes== {{notelist}} ==References== {{reflist|2}} ==External links== *[https://www.towatarijinjya.com/ Official website of Towatari Shrine] (Japanese) ‎]]]] {{Myōken Faith}} {{draft categories| [[Category:17th-century Shinto shrines]] [[Category:1600s establishments in Japan]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Chiba (city)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Shinbutsu shūgō]] [[Category:Shinbutsu bunri]] [[Category:Myōken Faith]] }} [[en:Towatari Shrine]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] {{Commons category|Towatari-jinja}} [[ja:登渡神社]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English revision.text:
[[commons:Category:Towatari-jinja]][[en:Towatari Shrine]][[ja:登渡神社]] [[commons:Category:Towatari-jinja]][[en:Towatari Shrine]][[ja:登渡神社]] [[sn:commons:Category:Towatari-jinja]][[en:Towatari Shrine]][[ja:登渡神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11579153]] Wikidata name: Towatari Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:02, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Shinto shrine in Chiba, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Towatari Shrine
登渡神社 | image = File:Towatari-jinja 06.jpg | caption = Towatari Shrine's ''[[Heiden (Shinto)|''hei'']]-[[Haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]'' | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|35.612306|N|140.106278|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|WD=Q2842779}}; {{ill|Amenohiwashi|WD=Q10939697}}; {{ill|Kamimusubi|WD=Q3275901}}; {{ill|Takamimusubi|WD=Q125400}} | founded_by = [[:ja:千葉定胤|Chiba Sadatane]] | established = [[:en:1644 (year)|1644]] ([[:en:Shōhō|Shōhō]] 1) | date_destroyed = | location = 3-3-8, Nobuto, [[:en:Chūō-ku, Chiba|Chūō-ku]], [[:en:Chiba (city)|Chiba]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]] | website = {{URL|https://www.towatarijinjya.com/}} | architecture_style = | festival = 4–6 September | leadership = | location = {{ill|Chuo|WD=Q1075869}} }} '''Towatari Shrine''' (登渡神社, ''Towatari-jinja''), also known as '''Nobuto Shrine''' (登戸神社, ''Nobuto-jinja''), is a [[Shinto]] [[:en:Shinto shrine|shrine]] located in Nobuto, [[:en:Chūō-ku, Chiba|Chūō-ku]], [[:en:Chiba (city)|Chiba City]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]], Japan. Originally a branch [[:en:Buddhist temples in Japan|temple]] of Kongojū-ji, a temple to the [[:en:Buddhism in Japan|Buddhist]] [[:en:Deva (Buddhism)|deity]] [[:en:Myōken|Myōken]] founded by the [[Chiba clan]] (modern [[Chiba Shrine]]), it was converted into a Shinto shrine during the mid-19th century. ==Deities== The shrine is dedicated to the three deities of creation (造化三神, ''zōka sanshin'') of classical [[Japanese mythology]]: [[Ame-no-Minakanushi]], [[Takamimusubi]], and [[Kamimusubi]]. Enshrined together with them is [[:ja:天日鷲神|1=Otori-sama|lt=Otori-sama|2=ja|3=天日鷲神|4=zh|5=天日鷲神|6=ko|7=아메노히와시노카미|Ame-no-Hiwashi]], originally the deity of a nearby shrine that was [[Shrine Consolidation Policy|merged]] into Nobuto Shrine in 1908 ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji]] 41).{{cite web|title=登渡神社について|url=https://www.towatarijinjya.com/jinjya|accessdate=2020-11-16|website=Towatari Shrine Official Website|language=Japanese}} ==History== [[File:Bay of Noboto.jpg|left|thumb|270x270px|''Bay of Noboto'' (Nobuto) by [[:en:Katsushika Hokusai|Katsushika Hokusai]]]] The shrine was originally founded in 1644 ([[:en:Shōhō|Shōhō]] 1) by [[:ja:千葉定胤|Chiba Sadatane]], 33rd head of the [[Chiba clan]], as a Buddhist temple named '''Hakujasan Shinkō-in Jōin-ji''' (白蛇山真光院定胤寺). It was a branch temple or ''matsuji'' (末寺) of Kongojū-ji (also known as Chiba Myōken-ji), a temple dedicated to [[:en:Myōken|Myōken]], the Buddhist deification of the [[:en:Big Dipper|Big Dipper]] and/or the [[:en:Polaris|northern pole star]] revered by the Chiba clan as their patron deity (what is today [[Chiba Shrine]]).{{cite book |editor=Chiba Prefecture |title=千葉県誌 巻上 (Chiba-ken shi, vol. 1) |date=1919 |publisher=Tadaya Shoten |pages=521 |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/960652/297}}{{cite book |last1=Nozaki |first1=Sabun |title=日本名勝地誌 第二編 (Nihon meishōchi shi, vol. 2) |date=1901 |publisher=Hakubunkan |page=166 |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=PkxMpcnBkZQC&dq=%E7%99%BB%E6%B8%A1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE&pg=PP200}}{{cite web |title=登渡神社 (Towatari-jinja) |url=http://www.jinja.ne.jp/ycDB/Board.cgi/001/db/ycDB-pc-jinja_detail.html?mode:search=1&search:method=1&search:field=jinja_id&search:expr=^$&search:word=1008 |website=神社のひろば 神社探検隊！ |publisher=Chiba-ken Jinjachō |accessdate=2020-11-18|language=Japanese}} In 1867 ([[:en:Keiō|Keiō]] 3), the temple became a Shinto shrine dedicated to the [[Kami|gods]] [[Ame-no-Minakanushi]] (a deity in Japanese mythology conflated with Myōken), [[Takamimusubi]], and [[Kamimusubi]] and renamed "Towatari Shrine". In 1990 ([[:en:Heisei|Heisei]] 2), the shrine's ''[[honden]]'' (dating from 1860) was moved to its present location and a new [[Heiden (Shinto)|''hei'']]-''[[Haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]'' constructed in front of it. ==Structures== *'''''Honden''''' :The ''honden'' was originally Jōin-ji's former [[:en:Main Hall (Japanese Buddhism)|main hall]], constructed in 1860 by a craftsman named Monjirō (紋次郎) from the [[:ja:八木村 (千葉県)|village of Yagi]] in [[:ja:葛飾郡|Katsushika District]], [[:en:Shimōsa Province|Shimōsa Province]] (part of the modern city of [[:en:Nagareyama|Nagareyama]], Chiba Prefecture).{{efn|Other notable projects by this artisan include the former main hall (the current Shaka-dō (釈迦堂) or Hall of [[:en:Gautama Buddha|Shakyamuni Tathāgata]]) of [[:en:Narita-san|Shinshō-ji]] in [[:en:Narita, Chiba|Narita]] (built 1858){{cite book |author1=Shiritsu Narita Toshokan |title=成田山通志 (Narita-san Tsushi) |date=1911 |publisher=Shiritsu Narita Toshokan |page=76 |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=QgNjpoXOSZMC}} and the former main hall of [[:ja:深川不動堂|Fukagawa Fudō-dō]] (a branch of Shinshō-ji) in [[:en:Fukagawa, Tokyo|Fukagawa]], [[:en:Kōtō|Kōtō City]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]] - originally a sanctuary dedicated to the [[:en:Bodhisattva|Bodhisattva]] [[:en:Kṣitigarbha|Kṣitigarbha]] (地蔵堂, Jizō-dō) in [[:ja:|Ryūfuku-ji]] in what is now [[:en:Inzai, Chiba|Inzai]] built somewhere between 1861 and 1864{{cite web |title=深川不動堂旧本堂 |url=https://www.city.koto.lg.jp/103020/bunkasports/bunka/bunkazaisiseki/kenzobutsu/17348.html |website=Kōtō City Official Website |language=Japanese}}{{cite web |title=今むかし④～本堂移築 |url=http://fukagawafudou.jugem.jp/?eid=314 |website=Narita-san Fukagawa Fudō-dō |accessdate=2020-11-18 |language=Japanese}}.}}{{cite book |editor=Chiba-shi Shi Hensan Iinkai |title=千葉市誌 (Chiba-shi Shi) |date=1958 |publisher=Chiba-shi Shi Hensan Iinkai |page=175}} The bas-reliefs decorating the building were carved by [[:ja:立川和四郎|Tatekawa Washirō Tomimasa]] (立川和四郎富昌, the 2nd generation of the Tatekawa Washirō line of craftsmen) from Suwa District in [[:en:Shinano Province|Shinano Province]] (modern [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]]) and/or his son Tomishige (富重).{{cite web |title=小壁嵌板彫刻（市指定文化財） |url=https://www.city.chiba.jp/kyoiku/shogaigakushu/bunkazai/kokabehameita.html |website=Chiba City Official Website |accessdate=2020-11-18 |language=Japanese}} The reliefs were designated [[Tangible Cultural Property (Japan)|tangible cultural properties]] by Chiba City in 1967 ([[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] 42). ==See also== *[[Chiba Shrine]] *[[Chiba clan]] *[[Taira clan]] *[[:en:Shimōsa Province|Shimōsa Province]] ==Notes== {{notelist}} ==References== {{reflist|2}} ==External links== *[https://www.towatarijinjya.com/ Official website of Towatari Shrine] (Japanese) ‎]]]] {{Myōken Faith}} {{draft categories| }} [[en:Towatari Shrine]] {{Commons category|Towatari-jinja}} [[ja:登渡神社]] [[commons:Category:Towatari-jinja]][[en:Towatari Shrine]][[ja:登渡神社]] [[commons:Category:Towatari-jinja]][[en:Towatari Shrine]][[ja:登渡神社]] [[sn:commons:Category:Towatari-jinja]][[en:Towatari Shrine]][[ja:登渡神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11579153]] Wikidata name: Towatari Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:02, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Shinto shrine in Chiba, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Towatari Shrine
登渡神社 | image = File:Towatari-jinja 06.jpg | caption = Towatari Shrine's ''[[Heiden (Shinto)|''hei'']]-[[Haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]'' | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|35.612306|N|140.106278|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|WD=Q2842779}}; {{ill|Amenohiwashi|WD=Q10939697}}; {{ill|Kamimusubi|WD=Q3275901}}; {{ill|Takamimusubi|WD=Q125400}} | founded_by = [[:ja:千葉定胤|Chiba Sadatane]] | established = [[:en:1644 (year)|1644]] ([[:en:Shōhō|Shōhō]] 1) | date_destroyed = | location = 3-3-8, Nobuto, [[:en:Chūō-ku, Chiba|Chūō-ku]], [[:en:Chiba (city)|Chiba]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]] | website = {{URL|https://www.towatarijinjya.com/}} | architecture_style = | festival = 4–6 September | leadership = | location = {{ill|Chuo|WD=Q1075869}} }} '''Towatari Shrine''' (登渡神社, ''Towatari-jinja''), also known as '''Nobuto Shrine''' (登戸神社, ''Nobuto-jinja''), is a [[Shinto]] [[:en:Shinto shrine|shrine]] located in Nobuto, [[:en:Chūō-ku, Chiba|Chūō-ku]], [[:en:Chiba (city)|Chiba City]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]], Japan. Originally a branch [[:en:Buddhist temples in Japan|temple]] of Kongojū-ji, a temple to the [[:en:Buddhism in Japan|Buddhist]] [[:en:Deva (Buddhism)|deity]] [[:en:Myōken|Myōken]] founded by the [[Chiba clan]] (modern [[Chiba Shrine]]), it was converted into a Shinto shrine during the mid-19th century. ==Deities== The shrine is dedicated to the three deities of creation (造化三神, ''zōka sanshin'') of classical [[Japanese mythology]]: [[Ame-no-Minakanushi]], [[Takamimusubi]], and [[Kamimusubi]]. Enshrined together with them is [[:ja:天日鷲神|1=Otori-sama|lt=Otori-sama|2=ja|3=天日鷲神|4=zh|5=天日鷲神|6=ko|7=아메노히와시노카미|Ame-no-Hiwashi]], originally the deity of a nearby shrine that was [[Shrine Consolidation Policy|merged]] into Nobuto Shrine in 1908 ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji]] 41).{{cite web|title=登渡神社について|url=https://www.towatarijinjya.com/jinjya|accessdate=2020-11-16|website=Towatari Shrine Official Website|language=Japanese}} ==History== [[File:Bay of Noboto.jpg|left|thumb|270x270px|''Bay of Noboto'' (Nobuto) by [[:en:Katsushika Hokusai|Katsushika Hokusai]]]] The shrine was originally founded in 1644 ([[:en:Shōhō|Shōhō]] 1) by [[:ja:千葉定胤|Chiba Sadatane]], 33rd head of the [[Chiba clan]], as a Buddhist temple named '''Hakujasan Shinkō-in Jōin-ji''' (白蛇山真光院定胤寺). It was a branch temple or ''matsuji'' (末寺) of Kongojū-ji (also known as Chiba Myōken-ji), a temple dedicated to [[:en:Myōken|Myōken]], the Buddhist deification of the [[:en:Big Dipper|Big Dipper]] and/or the [[:en:Polaris|northern pole star]] revered by the Chiba clan as their patron deity (what is today [[Chiba Shrine]]).{{cite book |editor=Chiba Prefecture |title=千葉県誌 巻上 (Chiba-ken shi, vol. 1) |date=1919 |publisher=Tadaya Shoten |pages=521 |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/960652/297}}{{cite book |last1=Nozaki |first1=Sabun |title=日本名勝地誌 第二編 (Nihon meishōchi shi, vol. 2) |date=1901 |publisher=Hakubunkan |page=166 |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=PkxMpcnBkZQC&dq=%E7%99%BB%E6%B8%A1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE&pg=PP200}}{{cite web |title=登渡神社 (Towatari-jinja) |url=http://www.jinja.ne.jp/ycDB/Board.cgi/001/db/ycDB-pc-jinja_detail.html?mode:search=1&search:method=1&search:field=jinja_id&search:expr=^$&search:word=1008 |website=神社のひろば 神社探検隊！ |publisher=Chiba-ken Jinjachō |accessdate=2020-11-18|language=Japanese}} In 1867 ([[:en:Keiō|Keiō]] 3), the temple became a Shinto shrine dedicated to the [[Kami|gods]] [[Ame-no-Minakanushi]] (a deity in Japanese mythology conflated with Myōken), [[Takamimusubi]], and [[Kamimusubi]] and renamed "Towatari Shrine". In 1990 ([[:en:Heisei|Heisei]] 2), the shrine's ''[[honden]]'' (dating from 1860) was moved to its present location and a new [[Heiden (Shinto)|''hei'']]-''[[Haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]'' constructed in front of it. ==Structures== *'''''Honden''''' :The ''honden'' was originally Jōin-ji's former [[:en:Main Hall (Japanese Buddhism)|main hall]], constructed in 1860 by a craftsman named Monjirō (紋次郎) from the [[:ja:八木村 (千葉県)|village of Yagi]] in [[:ja:葛飾郡|Katsushika District]], [[:en:Shimōsa Province|Shimōsa Province]] (part of the modern city of [[:en:Nagareyama|Nagareyama]], Chiba Prefecture).{{efn|Other notable projects by this artisan include the former main hall (the current Shaka-dō (釈迦堂) or Hall of [[:en:Gautama Buddha|Shakyamuni Tathāgata]]) of [[:en:Narita-san|Shinshō-ji]] in [[:en:Narita, Chiba|Narita]] (built 1858){{cite book |author1=Shiritsu Narita Toshokan |title=成田山通志 (Narita-san Tsushi) |date=1911 |publisher=Shiritsu Narita Toshokan |page=76 |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=QgNjpoXOSZMC}} and the former main hall of [[:ja:深川不動堂|Fukagawa Fudō-dō]] (a branch of Shinshō-ji) in [[:en:Fukagawa, Tokyo|Fukagawa]], [[:en:Kōtō|Kōtō City]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]] - originally a sanctuary dedicated to the [[:en:Bodhisattva|Bodhisattva]] [[:en:Kṣitigarbha|Kṣitigarbha]] (地蔵堂, Jizō-dō) in [[:ja:|Ryūfuku-ji]] in what is now [[:en:Inzai, Chiba|Inzai]] built somewhere between 1861 and 1864{{cite web |title=深川不動堂旧本堂 |url=https://www.city.koto.lg.jp/103020/bunkasports/bunka/bunkazaisiseki/kenzobutsu/17348.html |website=Kōtō City Official Website |language=Japanese}}{{cite web |title=今むかし④～本堂移築 |url=http://fukagawafudou.jugem.jp/?eid=314 |website=Narita-san Fukagawa Fudō-dō |accessdate=2020-11-18 |language=Japanese}}.}}{{cite book |editor=Chiba-shi Shi Hensan Iinkai |title=千葉市誌 (Chiba-shi Shi) |date=1958 |publisher=Chiba-shi Shi Hensan Iinkai |page=175}} The bas-reliefs decorating the building were carved by [[:ja:立川和四郎|Tatekawa Washirō Tomimasa]] (立川和四郎富昌, the 2nd generation of the Tatekawa Washirō line of craftsmen) from Suwa District in [[:en:Shinano Province|Shinano Province]] (modern [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]]) and/or his son Tomishige (富重).{{cite web |title=小壁嵌板彫刻（市指定文化財） |url=https://www.city.chiba.jp/kyoiku/shogaigakushu/bunkazai/kokabehameita.html |website=Chiba City Official Website |accessdate=2020-11-18 |language=Japanese}} The reliefs were designated [[Tangible Cultural Property (Japan)|tangible cultural properties]] by Chiba City in 1967 ([[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] 42). ==See also== *[[Chiba Shrine]] *[[Chiba clan]] *[[Taira clan]] *[[:en:Shimōsa Province|Shimōsa Province]] ==Notes== {{notelist}} ==References== {{reflist|2}} ==External links== *[https://www.towatarijinjya.com/ Official website of Towatari Shrine] (Japanese) ‎]]]] {{Myōken Faith}} {{draft categories| }} [[en:Towatari Shrine]] {{Commons category|Towatari-jinja}} [[ja:登渡神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11579153}} [[Category:1600s establishments in Japan]] [[Category:17th-century Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Chiba (city)]] [[Category:Myōken Faith]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Shinbutsu bunri]] [[Category:Shinbutsu shūgō]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]]

|editor=Chiba-shi Shi Hensan Iinkai |title=千葉市誌 (Chiba-shi Shi) |date=1958 |publisher=Chiba-shi Shi Hensan Iinkai |page=175}} The bas-reliefs decorating the building were carved by In 1990 ([[:en:Heisei|Heisei]] 2), the shrine's ''[[honden]]'' (dating from 1860) was moved to its present location and a new [[Heiden (Shinto)|''hei'']]-''[[Haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]'' constructed in front of it. ==Structures== *'''''Honden''''' :The ''honden'' was originally Jōin-ji's former [[:en:Main Hall (Japanese Buddhism)|main hall]], constructed in 1860 by a craftsman named Monjirō (紋次郎) from the [[:ja:八木村 (千葉県)|village of Yagi]] in [[:ja:葛飾郡|Katsushika District]], [[:en:Shimōsa Province|Shimōsa Province]] (part of the modern city of [[:en:Nagareyama|Nagareyama]], Chiba Prefecture).{{efn|Other notable projects by this artisan include the former main hall (the current Shaka-dō (釈迦堂) or Hall of [[:en:Gautama Buddha|Shakyamuni Tathāgata]]) of [[:en:Narita-san|Shinshō-ji]] in [[:en:Narita, Chiba|Narita]] (built 1858){{cite book |author1=Shiritsu Narita Toshokan |title=成田山通志 (Narita-san Tsushi) |date=1911 |publisher=Shiritsu Narita Toshokan |page=76 |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=QgNjpoXOSZMC}} and the former main hall of [[:ja:深川不動堂|Fukagawa Fudō-dō]] (a branch of Shinshō-ji) in [[:en:Fukagawa, Tokyo|Fukagawa]], [[:en:Kōtō|Kōtō City]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]] - originally a sanctuary dedicated to the [[:en:Bodhisattva|Bodhisattva]] [[:en:Kṣitigarbha|Kṣitigarbha]] (地蔵堂, Jizō-dō) in [[:ja:|Ryūfuku-ji]] in what is now [[:en:Inzai, Chiba|Inzai]] built somewhere between 1861 and 1864{{cite web |title=深川不動堂旧本堂 |url=https://www.city.koto.lg.jp/103020/bunkasports/bunka/bunkazaisiseki/kenzobutsu/17348.html |website=Kōtō City Official Website |language=Japanese}}{{cite web |title=今むかし④～本堂移築 |url=http://fukagawafudou.jugem.jp/?eid=314 |website=Narita-san Fukagawa Fudō-dō |accessdate=2020-11-18 |language=Japanese}}.}}{{cite book |editor=Chiba-shi Shi Hensan Iinkai |title=千葉市誌 (Chiba-shi Shi) |date=1958 |publisher=Chiba-shi Shi Hensan Iinkai |page=175}} The bas-reliefs decorating the building were carved by [[:ja:立川和四郎|Tatekawa Washirō Tomimasa]] (立川和四郎富昌, the 2nd generation of the Tatekawa Washirō line of craftsmen) from Suwa District in [[:en:Shinano Province|Shinano Province]] (modern [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]]) and/or his son Tomishige (富重).{{cite web |title=小壁嵌板彫刻（市指定文化財） |url=https://www.city.chiba.jp/kyoiku/shogaigakushu/bunkazai/kokabehameita.html |website=Chiba City Official Website |accessdate=2020-11-18 |language=Japanese}} The reliefs were designated [[Tangible Cultural Property (Japan)|tangible cultural properties]] by Chiba City in 1967 ([[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] 42). ==See also== *[[Chiba Shrine]] *[[Chiba clan]] *[[Taira clan]] *[[:en:Shimōsa Province|Shimōsa Province]] ==Notes== {{notelist}} ==References== {{reflist|2}} ==External links== *[https://www.towatarijinjya.com/ Official website of Towatari Shrine] (Japanese) ‎]]]] {{Myōken Faith}} [[en:Towatari Shrine]] {{Commons category|Towatari-jinja}} [[ja:登渡神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11579153}} [[Category:1600s establishments in Japan]] [[Category:17th-century Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Chiba (city)]] [[Category:Myōken Faith]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Shinbutsu bunri]] [[Category:Shinbutsu shūgō]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata [[co:commons:Category:Towatari-jinja]][[en:Towatari Shrine]][[ja:登渡神社]] [[co:commons:Category:Towatari-jinja]][[en:Towatari Shrine]][[ja:登渡神社]] [[sn:commons:Category:Towatari-jinja]][[en:Towatari Shrine]][[ja:登渡神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11579153]] Wikidata name: Towatari Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:02, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Shinto shrine in Chiba, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Towatari Shrine
登渡神社 | image = File:Towatari-jinja 06.jpg | caption = Towatari Shrine's ''[[Heiden (Shinto)|''hei'']]-[[Haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]'' | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|35.612306|N|140.106278|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|WD=Q2842779}}; {{ill|Amenohiwashi|WD=Q10939697}}; {{ill|Kamimusubi|WD=Q3275901}}; {{ill|Takamimusubi|WD=Q125400}} | founded_by = [[:ja:千葉定胤|Chiba Sadatane]] | established = [[:en:1644 (year)|1644]] ([[:en:Shōhō|Shōhō]] 1) | date_destroyed = | location = 3-3-8, Nobuto, [[:en:Chūō-ku, Chiba|Chūō-ku]], [[:en:Chiba (city)|Chiba]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]] | website = {{URL|https://www.towatarijinjya.com/}} | architecture_style = | festival = 4–6 September | leadership = | location = {{ill|Chuo|WD=Q1075869}} }} '''Towatari Shrine''' (登渡神社, ''Towatari-jinja''), also known as '''Nobuto Shrine''' (登戸神社, ''Nobuto-jinja''), is a [[Shinto]] [[:en:Shinto shrine|shrine]] located in Nobuto, [[:en:Chūō-ku, Chiba|Chūō-ku]], [[:en:Chiba (city)|Chiba City]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]], Japan. Originally a branch [[:en:Buddhist temples in Japan|temple]] of Kongojū-ji, a temple to the [[:en:Buddhism in Japan|Buddhist]] [[:en:Deva (Buddhism)|deity]] [[:en:Myōken|Myōken]] founded by the [[Chiba clan]] (modern [[Chiba Shrine]]), it was converted into a Shinto shrine during the mid-19th century. ==Deities== The shrine is dedicated to the three deities of creation (造化三神, ''zōka sanshin'') of classical [[Japanese mythology]]: [[Ame-no-Minakanushi]], [[Takamimusubi]], and [[Kamimusubi]]. Enshrined together with them is [[:ja:天日鷲神|1=Otori-sama|lt=Otori-sama|2=ja|3=天日鷲神|4=zh|5=天日鷲神|6=ko|7=아메노히와시노카미|Ame-no-Hiwashi]], originally the deity of a nearby shrine that was [[Shrine Consolidation Policy|merged]] into Nobuto Shrine in 1908 ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji]] 41).{{cite web|title=登渡神社について|url=https://www.towatarijinjya.com/jinjya|accessdate=2020-11-16|website=Towatari Shrine Official Website|language=Japanese}} ==History== [[File:Bay of Noboto.jpg|left|thumb|270x270px|''Bay of Noboto'' (Nobuto) by [[:en:Katsushika Hokusai|Katsushika Hokusai]]]] The shrine was originally founded in 1644 ([[:en:Shōhō|Shōhō]] 1) by [[:ja:千葉定胤|Chiba Sadatane]], 33rd head of the [[Chiba clan]], as a Buddhist temple named '''Hakujasan Shinkō-in Jōin-ji''' (白蛇山真光院定胤寺). It was a branch temple or ''matsuji'' (末寺) of Kongojū-ji (also known as Chiba Myōken-ji), a temple dedicated to [[:en:Myōken|Myōken]], the Buddhist deification of the [[:en:Big Dipper|Big Dipper]] and/or the [[:en:Polaris|northern pole star]] revered by the Chiba clan as their patron deity (what is today [[Chiba Shrine]]).{{cite book |editor=Chiba Prefecture |title=千葉県誌 巻上 (Chiba-ken shi, vol. 1) |date=1919 |publisher=Tadaya Shoten |pages=521 |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/960652/297}}{{cite book |last1=Nozaki |first1=Sabun |title=日本名勝地誌 第二編 (Nihon meishōchi shi, vol. 2) |date=1901 |publisher=Hakubunkan |page=166 |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=PkxMpcnBkZQC&dq=%E7%99%BB%E6%B8%A1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE&pg=PP200}}{{cite web |title=登渡神社 (Towatari-jinja) |url=http://www.jinja.ne.jp/ycDB/Board.cgi/001/db/ycDB-pc-jinja_detail.html?mode:search=1&search:method=1&search:field=jinja_id&search:expr=^$&search:word=1008 |website=神社のひろば 神社探検隊！ |publisher=Chiba-ken Jinjachō |accessdate=2020-11-18|language=Japanese}} In 1867 ([[:en:Keiō|Keiō]] 3), the temple became a Shinto shrine dedicated to the [[Kami|gods]] [[Ame-no-Minakanushi]] (a deity in Japanese mythology conflated with Myōken), [[Takamimusubi]], and [[Kamimusubi]] and renamed "Towatari Shrine". In 1990 ([[:en:Heisei|Heisei]] 2), the shrine's ''[[honden]]'' (dating from 1860) was moved to its present location and a new [[Heiden (Shinto)|''hei'']]-''[[Haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]'' constructed in front of it. ==Structures== *'''''Honden''''' :The ''honden'' was originally Jōin-ji's former [[:en:Main Hall (Japanese Buddhism)|main hall]], constructed in 1860 by a craftsman named Monjirō (紋次郎) from the [[:ja:八木村 (千葉県)|village of Yagi]] in [[:ja:葛飾郡|Katsushika District]], [[:en:Shimōsa Province|Shimōsa Province]] (part of the modern city of [[:en:Nagareyama|Nagareyama]], Chiba Prefecture).{{efn|Other notable projects by this artisan include the former main hall (the current Shaka-dō (釈迦堂) or Hall of [[:en:Gautama Buddha|Shakyamuni Tathāgata]]) of [[:en:Narita-san|Shinshō-ji]] in [[:en:Narita, Chiba|Narita]] (built 1858){{cite book |author1=Shiritsu Narita Toshokan |title=成田山通志 (Narita-san Tsushi) |date=1911 |publisher=Shiritsu Narita Toshokan |page=76 |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=QgNjpoXOSZMC}} and the former main hall of [[:ja:深川不動堂|Fukagawa Fudō-dō]] (a branch of Shinshō-ji) in [[:en:Fukagawa, Tokyo|Fukagawa]], [[:en:Kōtō|Kōtō City]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]] - originally a sanctuary dedicated to the [[:en:Bodhisattva|Bodhisattva]] [[:en:Kṣitigarbha|Kṣitigarbha]] (地蔵堂, Jizō-dō) in [[:ja:|Ryūfuku-ji]] in what is now [[:en:Inzai, Chiba|Inzai]] built somewhere between 1861 and 1864{{cite web |title=深川不動堂旧本堂 |url=https://www.city.koto.lg.jp/103020/bunkasports/bunka/bunkazaisiseki/kenzobutsu/17348.html |website=Kōtō City Official Website |language=Japanese}}{{cite web |title=今むかし④～本堂移築 |url=http://fukagawafudou.jugem.jp/?eid=314 |website=Narita-san Fukagawa Fudō-dō |accessdate=2020-11-18 |language=Japanese}}.}}{{cite book |editor=Chiba-shi Shi Hensan Iinkai |title=千葉市誌 (Chiba-shi Shi) |date=1958 |publisher=Chiba-shi Shi Hensan Iinkai |page=175}} The bas-reliefs decorating the building were carved by [[:ja:立川和四郎|Tatekawa Washirō Tomimasa]] (立川和四郎富昌, the 2nd generation of the Tatekawa Washirō line of craftsmen) from Suwa District in [[:en:Shinano Province|Shinano Province]] (modern [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]]) and/or his son Tomishige (富重).{{cite web |title=小壁嵌板彫刻（市指定文化財） |url=https://www.city.chiba.jp/kyoiku/shogaigakushu/bunkazai/kokabehameita.html |website=Chiba City Official Website |accessdate=2020-11-18 |language=Japanese}} The reliefs were designated [[Tangible Cultural Property (Japan)|tangible cultural properties]] by Chiba City in 1967 ([[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] 42). ==See also== *[[Chiba Shrine]] *[[Chiba clan]] *[[Taira clan]] *[[:en:Shimōsa Province|Shimōsa Province]] ==Notes== {{notelist}} ==References== {{reflist|2}} ==External links== *[https://www.towatarijinjya.com/ Official website of Towatari Shrine] (Japanese) ‎]]]] {{Myōken Faith}} [[en:Towatari Shrine]] {{Commons category|Towatari-jinja}} [[ja:登渡神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11579153}} [[Category:1600s establishments in Japan]] [[Category:17th-century Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Chiba (city)]] [[Category:Myōken Faith]] [[Category:Qq]] [[co:commons:Category:Towatari-jinja]][[en:Towatari Shrine]][[ja:登渡神社]] [[co:commons:Category:Towatari-jinja]][[en:Towatari Shrine]][[ja:登渡神社]] [[sn:commons:Category:Towatari-jinja]][[en:Towatari Shrine]][[ja:登渡神社]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11579153]] Wikidata name: Towatari Shrine [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 10:02, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Short description|Shinto shrine in Chiba, Japan}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Towatari Shrine
登渡神社 | image = File:Towatari-jinja 06.jpg | caption = Towatari Shrine's ''[[Heiden (Shinto)|''hei'']]-[[Haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]'' | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|35.612306|N|140.106278|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|WD=Q2842779}}; {{ill|Amenohiwashi|WD=Q10939697}}; {{ill|Kamimusubi|WD=Q3275901}}; {{ill|Takamimusubi|WD=Q125400}} | founded_by = [[:ja:千葉定胤|Chiba Sadatane]] | established = [[:en:1644 (year)|1644]] ([[:en:Shōhō|Shōhō]] 1) | date_destroyed = | location = 3-3-8, Nobuto, [[:en:Chūō-ku, Chiba|Chūō-ku]], [[:en:Chiba (city)|Chiba]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]] | website = {{URL|https://www.towatarijinjya.com/}} | architecture_style = | festival = 4–6 September | leadership = | location = {{ill|Chuo|WD=Q1075869}} }} '''Towatari Shrine''' (登渡神社, ''Towatari-jinja''), also known as '''Nobuto Shrine''' (登戸神社, ''Nobuto-jinja''), is a [[Shinto]] [[:en:Shinto shrine|shrine]] located in Nobuto, [[:en:Chūō-ku, Chiba|Chūō-ku]], [[:en:Chiba (city)|Chiba City]], [[:en:Chiba Prefecture|Chiba Prefecture]], Japan. Originally a branch [[:en:Buddhist temples in Japan|temple]] of Kongojū-ji, a temple to the [[:en:Buddhism in Japan|Buddhist]] [[:en:Deva (Buddhism)|deity]] [[:en:Myōken|Myōken]] founded by the [[Chiba clan]] (modern [[Chiba Shrine]]), it was converted into a Shinto shrine during the mid-19th century. ==Deities== The shrine is dedicated to the three deities of creation (造化三神, ''zōka sanshin'') of classical [[Japanese mythology]]: [[Ame-no-Minakanushi]], [[Takamimusubi]], and [[Kamimusubi]]. Enshrined together with them is [[:ja:天日鷲神|1=Otori-sama|lt=Otori-sama|2=ja|3=天日鷲神|4=zh|5=天日鷲神|6=ko|7=아메노히와시노카미|Ame-no-Hiwashi]], originally the deity of a nearby shrine that was [[Shrine Consolidation Policy|merged]] into Nobuto Shrine in 1908 ([[:en:Meiji era|Meiji]] 41).{{cite web|title=登渡神社について|url=https://www.towatarijinjya.com/jinjya|accessdate=2020-11-16|website=Towatari Shrine Official Website|language=Japanese}} ==History== [[File:Bay of Noboto.jpg|left|thumb|270x270px|''Bay of Noboto'' (Nobuto) by [[:en:Katsushika Hokusai|Katsushika Hokusai]]]] The shrine was originally founded in 1644 ([[:en:Shōhō|Shōhō]] 1) by [[:ja:千葉定胤|Chiba Sadatane]], 33rd head of the [[Chiba clan]], as a Buddhist temple named '''Hakujasan Shinkō-in Jōin-ji''' (白蛇山真光院定胤寺). It was a branch temple or ''matsuji'' (末寺) of Kongojū-ji (also known as Chiba Myōken-ji), a temple dedicated to [[:en:Myōken|Myōken]], the Buddhist deification of the [[:en:Big Dipper|Big Dipper]] and/or the [[:en:Polaris|northern pole star]] revered by the Chiba clan as their patron deity (what is today [[Chiba Shrine]]).{{cite book |editor=Chiba Prefecture |title=千葉県誌 巻上 (Chiba-ken shi, vol. 1) |date=1919 |publisher=Tadaya Shoten |pages=521 |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/960652/297}}{{cite book |last1=Nozaki |first1=Sabun |title=日本名勝地誌 第二編 (Nihon meishōchi shi, vol. 2) |date=1901 |publisher=Hakubunkan |page=166 |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=PkxMpcnBkZQC&dq=%E7%99%BB%E6%B8%A1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE&pg=PP200}}{{cite web |title=登渡神社 (Towatari-jinja) |url=http://www.jinja.ne.jp/ycDB/Board.cgi/001/db/ycDB-pc-jinja_detail.html?mode:search=1&search:method=1&search:field=jinja_id&search:expr=^$&search:word=1008 |website=神社のひろば 神社探検隊！ |publisher=Chiba-ken Jinjachō |accessdate=2020-11-18|language=Japanese}} In 1867 ([[:en:Keiō|Keiō]] 3), the temple became a Shinto shrine dedicated to the [[Kami|gods]] [[Ame-no-Minakanushi]] (a deity in Japanese mythology conflated with Myōken), [[Takamimusubi]], and [[Kamimusubi]] and renamed "Towatari Shrine". In 1990 ([[:en:Heisei|Heisei]] 2), the shrine's ''[[honden]]'' (dating from 1860) was moved to its present location and a new [[Heiden (Shinto)|''hei'']]-''[[Haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]'' constructed in front of it. ==Structures== *'''''Honden''''' :The ''honden'' was originally Jōin-ji's former [[:en:Main Hall (Japanese Buddhism)|main hall]], constructed in 1860 by a craftsman named Monjirō (紋次郎) from the [[:ja:八木村 (千葉県)|village of Yagi]] in [[:ja:葛飾郡|Katsushika District]], [[:en:Shimōsa Province|Shimōsa Province]] (part of the modern city of [[:en:Nagareyama|Nagareyama]], Chiba Prefecture).{{efn|Other notable projects by this artisan include the former main hall (the current Shaka-dō (釈迦堂) or Hall of [[:en:Gautama Buddha|Shakyamuni Tathāgata]]) of [[:en:Narita-san|Shinshō-ji]] in [[:en:Narita, Chiba|Narita]] (built 1858){{cite book |author1=Shiritsu Narita Toshokan |title=成田山通志 (Narita-san Tsushi) |date=1911 |publisher=Shiritsu Narita Toshokan |page=76 |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=QgNjpoXOSZMC}} and the former main hall of [[:ja:深川不動堂|Fukagawa Fudō-dō]] (a branch of Shinshō-ji) in [[:en:Fukagawa, Tokyo|Fukagawa]], [[:en:Kōtō|Kōtō City]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo]] - originally a sanctuary dedicated to the [[:en:Bodhisattva|Bodhisattva]] [[:en:Kṣitigarbha|Kṣitigarbha]] (地蔵堂, Jizō-dō) in [[:ja:|Ryūfuku-ji]] in what is now [[:en:Inzai, Chiba|Inzai]] built somewhere between 1861 and 1864{{cite web |title=深川不動堂旧本堂 |url=https://www.city.koto.lg.jp/103020/bunkasports/bunka/bunkazaisiseki/kenzobutsu/17348.html |website=Kōtō City Official Website |language=Japanese}}{{cite web |title=今むかし④～本堂移築 |url=http://fukagawafudou.jugem.jp/?eid=314 |website=Narita-san Fukagawa Fudō-dō |accessdate=2020-11-18 |language=Japanese}}.}}{{cite book |editor=Chiba-shi Shi Hensan Iinkai |title=千葉市誌 (Chiba-shi Shi) |date=1958 |publisher=Chiba-shi Shi Hensan Iinkai |page=175}} The bas-reliefs decorating the building were carved by [[:ja:立川和四郎|Tatekawa Washirō Tomimasa]] (立川和四郎富昌, the 2nd generation of the Tatekawa Washirō line of craftsmen) from Suwa District in [[:en:Shinano Province|Shinano Province]] (modern [[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]]) and/or his son Tomishige (富重).{{cite web |title=小壁嵌板彫刻（市指定文化財） |url=https://www.city.chiba.jp/kyoiku/shogaigakushu/bunkazai/kokabehameita.html |website=Chiba City Official Website |accessdate=2020-11-18 |language=Japanese}} The reliefs were designated [[Tangible Cultural Property (Japan)|tangible cultural properties]] by Chiba City in 1967 ([[:en:Shōwa era|Shōwa]] 42). ==See also== *[[Chiba Shrine]] *[[Chiba clan]] *[[Taira clan]] *[[:en:Shimōsa Province|Shimōsa Province]] ==Notes== {{notelist}} ==References== {{reflist|2}} ==External links== *[https://www.towatarijinjya.com/ Official website of Towatari Shrine] (Japanese) ‎]]]] {{Myōken Faith}} [[en:Towatari Shrine]] {{Commons category|Towatari-jinja}} [[ja:登渡神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11579153}} [[Category:1600s establishments in Japan]] [[Category:17th-century Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Chiba (city)]] [[Category:Myōken Faith]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]] [[Category:Shinbutsu bunri]] [[Category:Shinbutsu shūgō]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Chiba Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Pages with unclosed square brackets]]

登渡神社 | image = File:Towatari-jinja 06.jpg | caption = Towatari Shrine's ''[[Heiden (Shinto)|''hei'']]-[[Haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]'' | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|35.612306|N|140.106278|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|WD=Q2842779}}; {{ill|Amenohiwashi|WD=Q10939697}}; {{ill|Kamimusubi|WD=Q3275901}}; {{ill|Takamimusubi|WD=Q125400}} | founded_by = [[:ja:千葉定胤|Chiba Sadatane]] | established = [[:en:1644 (year)|1644]] ({{ill|Shōhō|en|Shōhō|WD=Q1146192|lt=Shōhō}} 1) | date_destroyed = | location = 3-3-8, Nobuto, {{ill|Chūō-ku, Chiba|en|Chūō-ku, Chiba|WD=Q1075869|lt=Chūō-ku}}, {{ill|Chiba (city)|en|Chiba (city)|WD=Q170616|lt=Chiba}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|WD=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture}} | website = {{URL|https://www.towatarijinjya.com/}} | architecture_style = | festival = 4–6 September | leadership = | location = {{ill|Chuo|WD=Q1075869}} }} '''Towatari Shrine''' (登渡神社, ''Towatari-jinja''), also known as '''Nobuto Shrine''' (登戸神社, ''Nobuto-jinja''), is a [[Shinto]] {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=shrine}} located in Nobuto, {{ill|Chūō-ku, Chiba|en|Chūō-ku, Chiba|WD=Q1075869|lt=Chūō-ku}}, {{ill|Chiba (city)|en|Chiba (city)|WD=Q170616|lt=Chiba City}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|WD=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture}}, Japan. Originally a branch {{ill|Buddhist temples in Japan|en|Buddhist temples in Japan|WD=Q3141900|lt=temple}} of Kongojū-ji, a temple to the {{ill|Buddhism in Japan|en|Buddhism in Japan|WD=Q736311|lt=Buddhist}} {{ill|Deva (Buddhism)|en|Deva (Buddhism)|WD=Q4089509|lt=deity}} {{ill|Myōken|en|Myōken|WD=Q10960623|lt=Myōken}} founded by the [[Chiba clan]] (modern [[Chiba Shrine]]), it was converted into a Shinto shrine during the mid-19th century. ==Deities== The shrine is dedicated to the three deities of creation (造化三神, ''zōka sanshin'') of classical [[Japanese mythology]]: [[Ame-no-Minakanushi]], [[Takamimusubi]], and [[Kamimusubi]]. Enshrined together with them is [[:ja:天日鷲神|1=Otori-sama|lt=Otori-sama|2=ja|3=天日鷲神|4=zh|5=天日鷲神|6=ko|7=아메노히와시노카미|Ame-no-Hiwashi]], originally the deity of a nearby shrine that was [[Shrine Consolidation Policy|merged]] into Nobuto Shrine in 1908 ({{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji}} 41).{{cite web|title=登渡神社について|url=https://www.towatarijinjya.com/jinjya|accessdate=2020-11-16|website=Towatari Shrine Official Website|language=Japanese}} ==History== [[File:Bay of Noboto.jpg|left|thumb|270x270px|''Bay of Noboto'' (Nobuto) by [[:en:Katsushika Hokusai|Katsushika Hokusai]]]] The shrine was originally founded in 1644 ({{ill|Shōhō|en|Shōhō|WD=Q1146192|lt=Shōhō}} 1) by [[:ja:千葉定胤|Chiba Sadatane]], 33rd head of the [[Chiba clan]], as a Buddhist temple named '''Hakujasan Shinkō-in Jōin-ji''' (白蛇山真光院定胤寺). It was a branch temple or ''matsuji'' (末寺) of Kongojū-ji (also known as Chiba Myōken-ji), a temple dedicated to {{ill|Myōken|en|Myōken|WD=Q10960623|lt=Myōken}}, the Buddhist deification of the {{ill|Big Dipper|en|Big Dipper|WD=Q10460|lt=Big Dipper}} and/or the {{ill|Polaris|en|Polaris|WD=Q12980|lt=northern pole star}} revered by the Chiba clan as their patron deity (what is today [[Chiba Shrine]]).{{cite book |editor=Chiba Prefecture |title=千葉県誌 巻上 (Chiba-ken shi, vol. 1) |date=1919 |publisher=Tadaya Shoten |pages=521 |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/960652/297}}{{cite book |last1=Nozaki |first1=Sabun |title=日本名勝地誌 第二編 (Nihon meishōchi shi, vol. 2) |date=1901 |publisher=Hakubunkan |page=166 |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=PkxMpcnBkZQC&dq=%E7%99%BB%E6%B8%A1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE&pg=PP200}}{{cite web |title=登渡神社 (Towatari-jinja) |url=http://www.jinja.ne.jp/ycDB/Board.cgi/001/db/ycDB-pc-jinja_detail.html?mode:search=1&search:method=1&search:field=jinja_id&search:expr=^$&search:word=1008 |website=神社のひろば 神社探検隊！ |publisher=Chiba-ken Jinjachō |accessdate=2020-11-18|language=Japanese}} In 1867 ({{ill|Keiō|en|Keiō|WD=Q776775|lt=Keiō}} 3), the temple became a Shinto shrine dedicated to the [[Kami|gods]] [[Ame-no-Minakanushi]] (a deity in Japanese mythology conflated with Myōken), [[Takamimusubi]], and [[Kamimusubi]] and renamed "Towatari Shrine". In 1990 ([[:en:Heisei|Heisei]] 2), the shrine's ''[[honden]]'' (dating from 1860) was moved to its present location and a new [[Heiden (Shinto)|''hei'']]-''[[Haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]'' constructed in front of it. ==Structures== *'''''Honden''''' :The ''honden'' was originally Jōin-ji's former {{ill|Main Hall (Japanese Buddhism)|en|Main Hall (Japanese Buddhism)|WD=Q17051413|lt=main hall}}, constructed in 1860 by a craftsman named Monjirō (紋次郎) from the [[:ja:八木村 (千葉県)|village of Yagi]] in [[:ja:葛飾郡|Katsushika District]], {{ill|Shimōsa Province|en|Shimōsa Province|WD=Q1053695|lt=Shimōsa Province}} (part of the modern city of {{ill|Nagareyama|en|Nagareyama|WD=Q328625|lt=Nagareyama}}, Chiba Prefecture).{{efn|Other notable projects by this artisan include the former main hall (the current Shaka-dō (釈迦堂) or Hall of [[:en:Gautama Buddha|Shakyamuni Tathāgata]]) of {{ill|Narita-san|en|Narita-san|WD=Q3083875|lt=Shinshō-ji}} in {{ill|Narita, Chiba|en|Narita, Chiba|WD=Q273798|lt=Narita}} (built 1858){{cite book |author1=Shiritsu Narita Toshokan |title=成田山通志 (Narita-san Tsushi) |date=1911 |publisher=Shiritsu Narita Toshokan |page=76 |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=QgNjpoXOSZMC}} and the former main hall of [[:ja:深川不動堂|Fukagawa Fudō-dō]] (a branch of Shinshō-ji) in {{ill|Fukagawa, Tokyo|en|Fukagawa, Tokyo|WD=Q3090689|lt=Fukagawa}}, {{ill|Kōtō|en|Kōtō|WD=Q215175|lt=Kōtō City}}, {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo}} - originally a sanctuary dedicated to the {{ill|Bodhisattva|en|Bodhisattva|WD=Q178149|lt=Bodhisattva}} {{ill|Kṣitigarbha|en|Kṣitigarbha|WD=Q604687|lt=Kṣitigarbha}} (地蔵堂, Jizō-dō) in [[:ja:|Ryūfuku-ji]] in what is now [[:en:Inzai, Chiba|Inzai]] built somewhere between 1861 and 1864{{cite web |title=深川不動堂旧本堂 |url=https://www.city.koto.lg.jp/103020/bunkasports/bunka/bunkazaisiseki/kenzobutsu/17348.html |website=Kōtō City Official Website |language=Japanese}}{{cite web |title=今むかし④～本堂移築 |url=http://fukagawafudou.jugem.jp/?eid=314 |website=Narita-san Fukagawa Fudō-dō |accessdate=2020-11-18 |language=Japanese}}.}}{{cite book |editor=Chiba-shi Shi Hensan Iinkai |title=千葉市誌 (Chiba-shi Shi) |date=1958 |publisher=Chiba-shi Shi Hensan Iinkai |page=175}} The bas-reliefs decorating the building were carved by [[:ja:立川和四郎|Tatekawa Washirō Tomimasa]] (立川和四郎富昌, the 2nd generation of the Tatekawa Washirō line of craftsmen) from Suwa District in {{ill|Shinano Province|en|Shinano Province|WD=Q289250|lt=Shinano Province}} (modern {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture}}) and/or his son Tomishige (富重).{{cite web |title=小壁嵌板彫刻（市指定文化財） |url=https://www.city.chiba.jp/kyoiku/shogaigakushu/bunkazai/kokabehameita.html |website=Chiba City Official Website |accessdate=2020-11-18 |language=Japanese}} The reliefs were designated [[Tangible Cultural Property (Japan)|tangible cultural properties]] by Chiba City in 1967 ({{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|WD=Q327567|lt=Shōwa}} 42). ==See also== *[[Chiba Shrine]] *[[Chiba clan]] *[[Taira clan]] 登渡神社 | image = File:Towatari-jinja 06.jpg | caption = Towatari Shrine's ''[[Heiden (Shinto)|''hei'']]-[[Haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]'' | map_type = Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{Coord|35.612306|N|140.106278|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|WD=Q2842779}}; {{ill|Amenohiwashi|WD=Q10939697}}; {{ill|Kamimusubi|WD=Q3275901}}; {{ill|Takamimusubi|WD=Q125400}} | founded_by = [[:ja:千葉定胤|Chiba Sadatane]] | established = [[:en:1644 (year)|1644]] ({{ill|Shōhō|en|Shōhō|WD=Q1146192|lt=Shōhō}} 1) | date_destroyed = | location = 3-3-8, Nobuto, {{ill|Chūō-ku, Chiba|en|Chūō-ku, Chiba|WD=Q1075869|lt=Chūō-ku}}, {{ill|Chiba (city)|en|Chiba (city)|WD=Q170616|lt=Chiba}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|WD=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture}} | website = {{URL|https://www.towatarijinjya.com/}} | architecture_style = | festival = 4–6 September | leadership = | location = {{ill|Chuo|WD=Q1075869}} }} '''Towatari Shrine''' (登渡神社, ''Towatari-jinja''), also known as '''Nobuto Shrine''' (登戸神社, ''Nobuto-jinja''), is a [[Shinto]] {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=shrine}} located in Nobuto, {{ill|Chūō-ku, Chiba|en|Chūō-ku, Chiba|WD=Q1075869|lt=Chūō-ku}}, {{ill|Chiba (city)|en|Chiba (city)|WD=Q170616|lt=Chiba City}}, {{ill|Chiba Prefecture|en|Chiba Prefecture|WD=Q80011|lt=Chiba Prefecture}}, Japan. Originally a branch {{ill|Buddhist temples in Japan|en|Buddhist temples in Japan|WD=Q3141900|lt=temple}} of Kongojū-ji, a temple to the {{ill|Buddhism in Japan|en|Buddhism in Japan|WD=Q736311|lt=Buddhist}} {{ill|Deva (Buddhism)|en|Deva (Buddhism)|WD=Q4089509|lt=deity}} {{ill|Myōken|en|Myōken|WD=Q10960623|lt=Myōken}} founded by the [[Chiba clan]] (modern [[Chiba Shrine]]), it was converted into a Shinto shrine during the mid-19th century. ==Deities== The shrine is dedicated to the three deities of creation (造化三神, ''zōka sanshin'') of classical [[Japanese mythology]]: [[Ame-no-Minakanushi]], [[Takamimusubi]], and [[Kamimusubi]]. Enshrined together with them is [[:ja:天日鷲神|1=Otori-sama|lt=Otori-sama|2=ja|3=天日鷲神|4=zh|5=天日鷲神|6=ko|7=아메노히와시노카미|Ame-no-Hiwashi]], originally the deity of a nearby shrine that was [[Shrine Consolidation Policy|merged]] into Nobuto Shrine in 1908 ({{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji}} 41).{{cite web|title=登渡神社について|url=https://www.towatarijinjya.com/jinjya|accessdate=2020-11-16|website=Towatari Shrine Official Website|language=Japanese}} ==History== [[File:Bay of Noboto.jpg|left|thumb|270x270px|''Bay of Noboto'' (Nobuto) by [[:en:Katsushika Hokusai|Katsushika Hokusai]]]] The shrine was originally founded in 1644 ({{ill|Shōhō|en|Shōhō|WD=Q1146192|lt=Shōhō}} 1) by [[:ja:千葉定胤|Chiba Sadatane]], 33rd head of the [[Chiba clan]], as a Buddhist temple named '''Hakujasan Shinkō-in Jōin-ji''' (白蛇山真光院定胤寺). It was a branch temple or ''matsuji'' (末寺) of Kongojū-ji (also known as Chiba Myōken-ji), a temple dedicated to {{ill|Myōken|en|Myōken|WD=Q10960623|lt=Myōken}}, the Buddhist deification of the {{ill|Big Dipper|en|Big Dipper|WD=Q10460|lt=Big Dipper}} and/or the {{ill|Polaris|en|Polaris|WD=Q12980|lt=northern pole star}} revered by the Chiba clan as their patron deity (what is today [[Chiba Shrine]]).{{cite book |editor=Chiba Prefecture |title=千葉県誌 巻上 (Chiba-ken shi, vol. 1) |date=1919 |publisher=Tadaya Shoten |pages=521 |url=https://dl.ndl.go.jp/info:ndljp/pid/960652/297}}{{cite book |last1=Nozaki |first1=Sabun |title=日本名勝地誌 第二編 (Nihon meishōchi shi, vol. 2) |date=1901 |publisher=Hakubunkan |page=166 |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=PkxMpcnBkZQC&dq=%E7%99%BB%E6%B8%A1%E7%A5%9E%E7%A4%BE&pg=PP200}}{{cite web |title=登渡神社 (Towatari-jinja) |url=http://www.jinja.ne.jp/ycDB/Board.cgi/001/db/ycDB-pc-jinja_detail.html?mode:search=1&search:method=1&search:field=jinja_id&search:expr=^$&search:word=1008 |website=神社のひろば 神社探検隊！ |publisher=Chiba-ken Jinjachō |accessdate=2020-11-18|language=Japanese}} In 1867 ({{ill|Keiō|en|Keiō|WD=Q776775|lt=Keiō}} 3), the temple became a Shinto shrine dedicated to the [[Kami|gods]] [[Ame-no-Minakanushi]] (a deity in Japanese mythology conflated with Myōken), [[Takamimusubi]], and [[Kamimusubi]] and renamed "Towatari Shrine". In 1990 ([[:en:Heisei|Heisei]] 2), the shrine's ''[[honden]]'' (dating from 1860) was moved to its present location and a new [[Heiden (Shinto)|''hei'']]-''[[Haiden (Shinto)|haiden]]'' constructed in front of it. ==Structures== *'''''Honden''''' :The ''honden'' was originally Jōin-ji's former {{ill|Main Hall (Japanese Buddhism)|en|Main Hall (Japanese Buddhism)|WD=Q17051413|lt=main hall}}, constructed in 1860 by a craftsman named Monjirō (紋次郎) from the [[:ja:八木村 (千葉県)|village of Yagi]] in [[:ja:葛飾郡|Katsushika District]], {{ill|Shimōsa Province|en|Shimōsa Province|WD=Q1053695|lt=Shimōsa Province}} (part of the modern city of {{ill|Nagareyama|en|Nagareyama|WD=Q328625|lt=Nagareyama}}, Chiba Prefecture).{{efn|Other notable projects by this artisan include the former main hall (the current Shaka-dō (釈迦堂) or Hall of [[:en:Gautama Buddha|Shakyamuni Tathāgata]]) of {{ill|Narita-san|en|Narita-san|WD=Q3083875|lt=Shinshō-ji}} in {{ill|Narita, Chiba|en|Narita, Chiba|WD=Q273798|lt=Narita}} (built 1858){{cite book |author1=Shiritsu Narita Toshokan |title=成田山通志 (Narita-san Tsushi) |date=1911 |publisher=Shiritsu Narita Toshokan |page=76 |url=https://books.google.com/books?id=QgNjpoXOSZMC}} and the former main hall of [[:ja:深川不動堂|Fukagawa Fudō-dō]] (a branch of Shinshō-ji) in {{ill|Fukagawa, Tokyo|en|Fukagawa, Tokyo|WD=Q3090689|lt=Fukagawa}}, {{ill|Kōtō|en|Kōtō|WD=Q215175|lt=Kōtō City}}, {{ill|Tokyo|en|Tokyo|WD=Q1490|lt=Tokyo}} - originally a sanctuary dedicated to the {{ill|Bodhisattva|en|Bodhisattva|WD=Q178149|lt=Bodhisattva}} {{ill|Kṣitigarbha|en|Kṣitigarbha|WD=Q604687|lt=Kṣitigarbha}} (地蔵堂, Jizō-dō) in [[:ja:|Ryūfuku-ji]] in what is now [[:en:Inzai, Chiba|Inzai]] built somewhere between 1861 and 1864{{cite web |title=深川不動堂旧本堂 |url=https://www.city.koto.lg.jp/103020/bunkasports/bunka/bunkazaisiseki/kenzobutsu/17348.html |website=Kōtō City Official Website |language=Japanese}}{{cite web |title=今むかし④～本堂移築 |url=http://fukagawafudou.jugem.jp/?eid=314 |website=Narita-san Fukagawa Fudō-dō |accessdate=2020-11-18 |language=Japanese}}.}}{{cite book |editor=Chiba-shi Shi Hensan Iinkai |title=千葉市誌 (Chiba-shi Shi) |date=1958 |publisher=Chiba-shi Shi Hensan Iinkai |page=175}} The bas-reliefs decorating the building were carved by [[:ja:立川和四郎|Tatekawa Washirō Tomimasa]] (立川和四郎富昌, the 2nd generation of the Tatekawa Washirō line of craftsmen) from Suwa District in {{ill|Shinano Province|en|Shinano Province|WD=Q289250|lt=Shinano Province}} (modern {{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture}}) and/or his son Tomishige (富重).{{cite web |title=小壁嵌板彫刻（市指定文化財） |url=https://www.city.chiba.jp/kyoiku/shogaigakushu/bunkazai/kokabehameita.html |website=Chiba City Official Website |accessdate=2020-11-18 |language=Japanese}} The reliefs were designated [[Tangible Cultural Property (Japan)|tangible cultural properties]] by Chiba City in 1967 ({{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|WD=Q327567|lt=Shōwa}} 42). ==See also== *[[Chiba Shrine]] *[[Chiba clan]] *[[Taira clan]] *{{ill|Shimōsa Province|en|Shimōsa Province|WD=Q1053695|lt=Shimōsa Province}} ==Notes== {{notelist}} ==References== {{reflist|2}} ==External links== *[https://www.towatarijinjya.com/ Official website of Towatari Shrine] (Japanese) ‎]]]] {{Myōken Faith}} [[en:Towatari Shrine]] {{Commons category|Towatari-jinja}} [[ja:登渡神社]] {{wikidata link|Q11579153}} [[Category:1600s establishments in Japan]] [[Category:17th-century Shinto shrines]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Chiba (city)]] [[Category:Myōken Faith]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Shinbutsu bunri]] [[Category:Shinbutsu shūgō]]
